calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-23-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Terri Knight.
Megan Irish appeared by zoom.

Ms. Irish stated that she was Counsel in a personal injury matter in which she represented Mattie Duncan prior to her passing, that she has money sitting in her attorney client trust account and that she needs to know where the money is going to go. The Court noted that at the last calling of the case the matter was continued to allow Timothy Knight the opportunity to provide evidence in support of his claim, that it issued an order setting a briefing schedule with instructions to Mr. Knight to file his documents and that no documents have been received from Mr. Knight. Mr. Vann stated that he has had no contact from Timothy Knight. The Court instructed Mr. Vann to pay the outstanding copy fees and stated that Mr. Vann does not have to pay for Timothy Knight's waived fees. Mr. Vann stated that he will provide instructions to Ms. Irish. The First Amended First and Final Account, etc. is under submission with the Court's intent to grant the petition.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-20-2020 Continued To Sep-14-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Terri Knight.
Megan Irish appeared by zoom.

Ms. Irish stated that she represented Mattie Duncan in an unrelated case and that she has funds in her Attorney Trust Account that need to be disbursed to the estate. Mr. Vann stated that Timothy Knight is expected to be released in February. The Court raised the issue of the difficulty of incarcerated parties' ability to have access to the Court, stated that it wants to give Mr. Knight an opportunity to appear and asked if there is any prospect of settling this. Mr. Vann stated that there are two pieces of property, that the property on Ralston Street in San Francisco went to his Client and that the other property in Pennsylvania went to his Client's Brother (Timothy). Ms. Knight stated that Timothy never lived in the Pennsylvania property, that Timothy rented it out to family members, that the family members destroyed the property and that she received notice that the property was going to be auctioned off. Ms. Knight also stated that she stepped in and paid over $2,000.00 to save the property, that her Brother was incarcerated at the time, that he got out and worked with an Agent to sell the property, and the he only received $7,000.00 from the sale, but he didn't pay back her money. The First Amended First and Final Account, etc. is continued to September 14, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Vann is to send a letter to Mr. Knight indicating that he has six weeks to provide evidence in support of his claim. Said evidence is to be filed by August 31, 2020. Mr. Vann is to file a response by September 8,2020. The Court informed Ms. Irish that it is unable to assist her at this time.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-29-2020 Continued To Jul-20-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dennis Vann for and with Terri Knight.

The Court asks if there is any chance of working this out. Mr. Vann stated that he did not receive the objection from Timothy Knight, that Mr. Knight is alleging that there is some type of agreement between him (Timothy) and Terri to waives fees, and that there is no such agreement. Mr. Vann also stated that Mr. Knight is incarcerated, and that notice was given to Mr. Knight of the first hearing, but that he did not provide notice of today's hearing. The Court expressed concern about Mr. Knight not being aware of today's hearing and not being aware of the ability to appear telephonically. The Probate Examiner informed Mr. Vann about the Probate 5th amended general order with respect to the requirement of Counsel for the moving Party to inform all Parties of the courtcall procedure. Ms. Knight stated that she does not understand what the 2005 Father's estate has to do with this matter/her Mom's estate and that the $19,000.00 has to do with this right here, not the 2005 matter. The First Amended First and Final Account, etc. is continued to July 20, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The notice is to inform Mr. Knight of the availability of and how to appear by courtcall.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Close Of Estate Set
Off Calendar Nov-20-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Close Of Estate Set
Off Calendar Oct-23-2019 Continued To Nov-20-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dennis Vann for and with Terri Knight (Administrator).

The Court stated at the last hearing that Mr. Vann was not present, that Ms. Knight was present, and that it is still looking for the petition for final distribution. Mr. Vann stated that he'd spoken with Ms. Knight, that Ms. Knight had consulted with another attorney by the name of Vincent D'Angelo, that he'd spoken with Mr. D'Angelo, and that there is only $48,000.00 left from the close of escrow. Ms. Knight addressed the Court. Mr. Vann stated that the estate is ready to close. The Court addressed Mr. Vann stating that he is still counsel of record and that a substitution of attorney is not on file. The Court addressed Ms. Knight stating that whom she chooses to work with is her decision and that she can work with Mr. Vann or she needs to get new counsel in right away. The Court then continued the status on close of estate to November 12, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 stating that it will be looking for a petition for final distribution or a petition to approve status on administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: M. Nuque. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Close Of Estate Set
Off Calendar Sep-25-2019 Continued To Oct-23-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christina Garcia for and with Terri Knight.

Ms. Garcia stated that she is from the Dolan Law Firm, that she is making a special appearance for Ms. Knight, that Ms. Knight is represented by Dennis Vann, but Ms. Knight has been unable to reach him, and that her office has also reached out to Mr. Vann and has not heard back. The Court noted that the letters were issued in 2018, that this date was set at the same time and reminds Ms. Knight that it is ultimately her responsibility to make sure that things are getting done. Ms. Knight stated that she would like to seek other representation. Status on close of estate is continued to October 23, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court informed Ms. Knight that she has to file either a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration no later than October 16, 2019 (one week prior to the hearing).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
